title: The trans-Planckian problem of Hawking radiation
statement: In the standard derivation, the Hawking quanta seen at late times are traced back to modes that were, near the horizon at early times, blueshifted to arbitrarily high (trans-Planckian) frequencies — far beyond where the underlying field theory and the fixed-background approximation can be trusted. The trans-Planckian problem asks whether Hawking radiation is a genuine, robust prediction or an artifact of extrapolating physics into an unknown ultraviolet regime. Analogue-gravity systems and modified-dispersion models give strong evidence for robustness, but a fully satisfactory, first-principles demonstration awaits the true short-distance theory.

originators.name: William Unruh
originators.note: 1981 raised the issue and proposed the sonic (analogue) horizon as a testbed.

originators.name: Ted Jacobson
originators.note: 1991 analysis of Hawking radiation and ultrashort distances / modified dispersion.

ranking.rationale: Less deep than the information cluster (it questions an approximation, not unitarity) and unusually tractable: analogue-gravity experiments and dispersion models let it be probed concretely; hence ranked lower.

key_ideas: Near-horizon exponential blueshift sends the origin of late Hawking quanta to trans-Planckian frequencies in the naive calculation.
Modified-dispersion-relation models (introducing a UV cutoff) still reproduce a thermal spectrum to good approximation — evidence of robustness.
Analogue gravity: sonic/optical/BEC 'horizons' realize the same kinematics with KNOWN microphysics, providing experimental tests.
Observation of stimulated and (claimed) spontaneous analogue Hawking radiation in BECs (Steinhauer) supports the effect's robustness.
The result hints that Hawking radiation is a low-energy, horizon-kinematics phenomenon insensitive to UV details — but this is argued, not proven.
Connects to whether the equivalence principle plus QFT alone fix the radiation, independent of quantum gravity.

approaches.name: Modified dispersion relations
approaches.summary: Impose a Lorentz-violating UV cutoff and check the spectrum survives.

approaches.name: Analogue gravity (sonic/optical/BEC horizons)
approaches.summary: Reproduce horizon kinematics in condensed-matter systems with known microphysics; measure analogue Hawking radiation.

approaches.name: Wave-packet / mode-tracking analyses
approaches.summary: Track the relevant modes carefully to show the trans-Planckian region is not essential.

papers.title: Particle Creation by Black Holes
papers.note: Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199-220 (1975). The original derivation of black-hole thermal emission; the infinite blueshift of outgoing modes traced backward to the horizon is the very feature that creates the trans-Planckian problem.

papers.title: Experimental Black-Hole Evaporation?
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 46, 1351 (1981). Founding paper of analogue gravity: sound waves in a transonic fluid flow form a sonic horizon emitting thermal phonons, opening the route to laboratory tests of the Hawking effect's robustness.

papers.title: Black-hole evaporation and ultrashort distances
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 44, 1731 (1991). One of the first explicit formulations of the trans-Planckian problem, arguing Hawking's derivation relies on arbitrarily short-distance (trans-Planckian) field modes.

papers.title: Sonic analog of black holes and the effects of high frequencies on black hole evaporation
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 51, 2827 (1995). Showed via a modified (dispersive) dispersion relation in the sonic-analogue model that the thermal Hawking spectrum is largely insensitive to high-frequency/short-distance physics — the first quantitative robustness result.

papers.title: Hawking radiation without trans-Planckian frequencies
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 52, 4559 (1995). Analytic treatment of Hawking emission with a dispersive cutoff showing the thermal flux can be recovered without invoking trans-Planckian modes; a foundational robustness analysis (Brout et al.).

papers.title: Hawking radiation and the trans-Planckian problem in dispersive models / Hawking spectrum and high frequency dispersion
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 54, 1568 (1996). Canonical lattice/dispersive-field computation confirming the near-thermal Hawking spectrum survives a modified high-frequency dispersion relation; the standard reference for the Corley-Jacobson method.

papers.title: Generalization of the model of Hawking radiation with modified high frequency dispersion relation
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D. Extends Corley's method to general choices of preferred frame, showing the thermal spectrum at the Hawking temperature is recovered with corrections of order k0^-2.

papers.title: kappa-Poincare dispersion relations and the black hole radiation
papers.note: Physics Letters B. Applies the Corley-Jacobson approach to a quantum-gravity-motivated kappa-Poincare dispersion relation (sub- and superluminal).

papers.title: Hawking Radiation in the Laboratory
papers.note: Progress of Theoretical Physics. Proposes a Laval-nozzle sonic analogue and a classical power-spectrum treatment to make Hawking-analogue experiments feasible.

papers.title: Analogue Gravity
papers.note: Living Reviews in Relativity (1230+ citations). The standard comprehensive review of analogue models of gravity, including the dispersion/robustness and trans-Planckian discussions. (Updated 2011 edition also exists.)

papers.title: Nonlocal density correlations as a signature of Hawking radiation from acoustic black holes
papers.note: Physical Review A (141 citations). Proposed the density-density correlation function as the observable signature of analogue Hawking pairs in a BEC — the method used in later experiments.

papers.title: Numerical observation of Hawking radiation from acoustic black holes in atomic Bose-Einstein condensates
papers.note: New Journal of Physics (231 citations). Microscopic Gross-Pitaevskii simulation confirming analogue Hawking emission via long-range density correlations and its robustness against finite temperature.

papers.title: Measurement of stimulated Hawking emission in an analogue system
papers.note: Physical Review Letters (471 citations). Landmark water-wave (white-hole) experiment demonstrating the thermal nature of the classical/stimulated Hawking conversion process.

papers.title: Black hole radiation with short distance dispersion, an analytical S-matrix approach
papers.note: Physical Review D (72 citations). Connection-formula/S-matrix derivation explaining the robustness of the Hawking spectrum and two-point function under short-distance dispersion, including black-hole-laser implications.

papers.title: Observation of self-amplifying Hawking radiation in an analogue black-hole laser
papers.note: Nature Physics (323 citations). Reported self-amplifying (black-hole-laser) analogue Hawking radiation between two horizons in a BEC.

papers.title: Mechanism of stimulated Hawking radiation in a laboratory Bose-Einstein condensate
papers.note: Physical Review A. Gross-Pitaevskii model reproducing the Steinhauer 2014 experiment and clarifying its hydrodynamic/mean-field mechanism (a critical reassessment of the self-amplification claim).

papers.title: Observation of quantum Hawking radiation and its entanglement in an analogue black hole
papers.note: Nature Physics (571 citations). Reported spontaneous, quantum (entangled) analogue Hawking radiation in a BEC with an approximately thermal spectrum — the central modern experimental claim, and directly relevant to robustness against the trans-Planckian problem.

papers.title: Questioning the Recent Observation of Quantum Hawking Radiation
papers.note: Annalen der Physik (36 citations). A prominent critique questioning the statistical significance and theoretical consistency of the Steinhauer 2016 observation — captures the live controversy.

papers.title: Stochastically Fluctuating Black-Hole Geometry, Hawking Radiation and the Trans-Planckian Problem
papers.note: Physical Review D (58 citations). Argues metric (geometry) fluctuations mimic a modified dispersion relation, suggesting a physical origin for the dispersive cutoff that tames the trans-Planckian reservoir.

papers.title: Avoiding the Trans-Planckian Problem in Black Hole Physics
papers.note: Uses avoidance of the trans-Planckian problem as a guiding principle to classify possible black-hole evaporation scenarios.

papers.title: Ramp-up of Hawking Radiation in Bose-Einstein-Condensate Analog Black Holes
papers.note: Physical Review Letters (19 citations). Analytic model of acoustic black-hole formation in a BEC showing analogue Hawking radiation emerges from a 'quantum atmosphere' displaced from the horizon, at T=0 and finite T.

papers.title: Impact of trans-Planckian excitations on black-hole radiation in dipolar condensates
papers.note: Physical Review D (23 citations). Shows a roton-minimum (trans-Planckian) feature in a dipolar-BEC dispersion relation strongly imprints on the Hawking spectrum, including emission even without a horizon — a direct probe of UV sensitivity.

papers.title: Hawking radiation in Lorentz violating gravity: a tale of two horizons
papers.note: Journal of High Energy Physics (11 citations). Shows species-independent thermal emission from universal horizons in Lorentz-violating gravity, with the low-energy spectrum mimicking the standard Hawking result — robustness under explicit Lorentz breaking.

papers.title: UV dispersive effects on Hawking radiation
papers.note: Physical Review D. Finds that for non-monotonic dispersion relations differing only in the trans-Planckian domain, the Hawking amplitude can be significantly modified after the scrambling time even though the temperature is unchanged — challenges the naive UV-insensitivity belief.

papers.title: Tunneling method for Hawking quanta in analogue gravity
papers.note: Comptes Rendus Physique (15 citations). Unified tunneling-method treatment clarifying the physical origin and limits of Hawking-effect robustness under sub-/superluminal modified dispersion relations.

physicists.name: Stephen Hawking
physicists.contribution: Predicted black-hole thermal radiation (1975); his derivation's reliance on infinitely blueshifted modes is the source of the trans-Planckian problem.

physicists.name: William G. Unruh
physicists.contribution: Founded analogue gravity (1981) and showed (1995) the Hawking spectrum is largely robust against high-frequency modifications via the sonic-analogue dispersive model.

physicists.name: Ted Jacobson
physicists.contribution: Sharpened the trans-Planckian problem (1991) and co-developed the Corley-Jacobson lattice/dispersive computation of the modified Hawking spectrum.

physicists.name: Renaud Parentani
physicists.contribution: Leading theorist of dispersive/analogue Hawking radiation; co-author of the Brout-Massar-Parentani-Spindel robustness analyses and S-matrix treatments of short-distance dispersion.

physicists.name: Matt Visser
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the standard 'Analogue Gravity' Living Reviews; developed the acoustic-metric formalism underpinning analogue Hawking studies.

physicists.name: Stefano Liberati
physicists.contribution: Co-author of 'Analogue Gravity'; active on Hawking-effect robustness under Lorentz violation, universal horizons, and tunneling methods in dispersive settings.

physicists.name: Carlos Barcelo
physicists.contribution: Lead author of the 'Analogue Gravity' reviews; works on using avoidance of the trans-Planckian problem as a guide to black-hole evaporation scenarios.

physicists.name: Jeff Steinhauer
physicists.contribution: Performed the headline BEC analogue-black-hole experiments (2014 black-hole laser; 2016 quantum/entangled Hawking radiation) testing the effect's robustness in the lab.

physicists.name: Iacopo Carusotto
physicists.affiliation: INO-CNR BEC Center, University of Trento
physicists.contribution: Pioneered numerical (Gross-Pitaevskii) simulations of analogue Hawking radiation and the density-correlation signature used to detect it.

physicists.name: Silke Weinfurtner
physicists.contribution: Led the water-wave (white-hole) experiments demonstrating the thermal/stimulated Hawking conversion, probing the classical robustness of the effect.

physicists.name: Ulf Leonhardt
physicists.contribution: Optical-analogue (fiber/horizon) Hawking-radiation researcher and prominent critic scrutinizing the statistical significance of analogue-Hawking observation claims.

simulator_angle: The near-horizon blueshift is exactly the gravitational-redshift factor the simulator already computes; the simulator can visualize how the redshift diverges approaching the horizon, the kinematic root of the trans-Planckian issue.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 25 verified, genuinely-relevant papers and 11 physicists (lightly over the ~10 target; all real). Method: ran 7 batches (21 sub-queries) on the Consensus search MCP across the four themes (trans-Planckian problem, modified dispersion/robustness, analogue gravity sonic horizons, BEC/water-wave experiments incl. Steinhauer), pacing 3 calls at a time with ~35s waits to respect rate limits. 18 papers carry live Consensus URLs from those searches; 7 are canonical landmarks I cite with certainty from bibliographic data (Hawking 1975, Unruh 1981 & 1995, Jacobson 1991, Brout-Massar-Parentani-Spindel 1995, Corley-Jacobson 1996) with DOIs/journal notes rather than search URLs. The DOIs given for the classics are the standard published DOIs for those well-known articles; the Corley-Jacobson entry pairs the canonical PRD 54 1568 reference with the standard method title. Tiers reflect that this is an OPEN problem: robustness/resolution claims are open_conjecture or numerical_approximation, lab results are observational_constraint, and exact GR (Hawking, Unruh 1981) is analytic_classical. I deliberately did not pad — every entry is on-topic for the trans-Planckian problem and its analogue/dispersive resolutions. Note: I did not independently re-verify each classic's DOI against a live registry in this session, so a downstream check of the 7 hand-supplied identifiers is advisable.
